name: Southeast and Battle of Arapiles Route
name_original: Calvarrasa de Arriba y Alba de Tormes — circular por Peñaranda de Bracamonte
description: This moderate 34.8-mile mountain biking route near Salamanca offers scenic views of the Castilian countryside and historic Alba de Tormes.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Calvarrasa De Arriba is primarily found within the Campo Charro region, characterized by varied natural settings. The area features the Ruta de la Plata Greenway, a 12.6-mile (20.2 km) natural trail built on a disused railway line, offering accessible routes. Riders can expect to traverse holm oak dehesas, expansive cereal steppes, and riverside ecosystems along the Tormes River. The broader Salamanca province provides a network of mountain biking routes catering to various difficulty levels, from gentle paths to more challenging mountain trails. Best mountain bike trails around Calvarrasa De Arriba The most popular mountain bike trail is Southeast and Battle of Arapiles Route, a 34.8 miles (56.1 km) moderate trail that takes 4 hours 15 minutes to complete. This route passes near the historical site of the Battle of Salamanca at Los Arapiles. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Nature – Banks of the Tormes loop from Centro Reto, a moderate 29.3 miles (47.1 km) path. This route explores the riverside ecosystems along the Tormes River. Local mountain bikers also love the View of Salamanca Cathedral – Roman Bridge of Salamanca loop from Carbajosa de la Sagrada, a 32.5 miles (52.3 km) trail leading through varied rural landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 39 minutes. Mountain biking around Calvarrasa De Arriba is defined by holm oak dehesas, cereal steppes, and riverside ecosystems, with the Ruta de la Plata Greenway providing a central accessible route. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy family rides to more extensive moderate trails. The routes in Calvarrasa De Arriba are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 2000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Calvarrasa De Arriba's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Calvarrasa de Arriba?
A: There are over 200 mountain bike trails around Calvarrasa de Arriba, offering a wide range of options for all skill levels. This includes 40 easy routes, 161 moderate routes, and 14 difficult routes.

Q: Are there easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Calvarrasa de Arriba is home to the Ruta de la Plata Greenway, a 20.2-kilometer natural trail built on a disused railway line. This trail offers an accessible and scenic route with hardly any difficulties, making it ideal for family outings and leisurely rides. There are 40 easy routes in total.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Calvarrasa de Arriba?
A: The trails in Calvarrasa de Arriba traverse diverse landscapes characteristic of the Campo Charro region. You can expect to ride through serene holm oak dehesas, expansive cereal steppes, and lush riverside ecosystems, particularly along the Tormes River. The Ruta de la Plata Greenway also offers varied scenery.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: While the immediate area around Calvarrasa de Arriba features many accessible routes, the broader Salamanca province offers a network of mountain biking routes that cater to various difficulty levels, including more challenging terrains. There are 14 difficult routes available in the region for experienced riders seeking a greater challenge.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes in Calvarrasa de Arriba?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31859945" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nature – Banks of the Tormes loop from Carbajosa de la Sagrada&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 31.9-mile (51.5 km) path that explores riverside ecosystems. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33769289" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Salamanca Cathedral – Roman Bridge of Salamanca loop from Carbajosa de la Sagrada&lt;/a&gt;, a 26.6-mile (42.7 km) trail.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The region offers a blend of natural beauty and historical significance. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1190995775" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Southeast and Battle of Arapiles Route&lt;/a&gt; passes near the historical site of the Battle of Salamanca at Los Arapiles, where an interpretation panel and monument commemorate the famous battle. You can also find a 1st-century square bullring within Calvarrasa de Arriba itself. Further afield, you might encounter landmarks like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1392032" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge of Salamanca&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Calvarrasa de Arriba?
A: The mountain bike trails around Calvarrasa de Arriba are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 200 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, from holm oak dehesas to riverside paths, and the accessibility of routes like the Ruta de la Plata Greenway.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Calvarrasa de Arriba?
A: The region generally offers pleasant conditions for mountain biking for much of the year. Spring and autumn typically provide mild temperatures and beautiful scenery, making them ideal. Summers can be hot, so early mornings or late afternoons are preferable, while winters are generally mild but can have cooler, wetter days.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of Salamanca?
A: Yes, some routes provide scenic views towards the city of Salamanca. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33769289" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Salamanca Cathedral – Roman Bridge of Salamanca loop from Carbajosa de la Sagrada&lt;/a&gt; is a notable example, offering glimpses of the iconic Salamanca Cathedral and other city landmarks as you ride through varied rural landscapes.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails that follow riverbanks?
A: Absolutely. The area is known for its riverside ecosystems along the Tormes River. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31859945" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nature – Banks of the Tormes loop from Carbajosa de la Sagrada&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate route that specifically explores these lush green areas, offering a refreshing riding experience.

Q: How long do mountain bike trails typically take to complete?
A: Trail durations vary significantly based on distance and difficulty. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1190995775" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Southeast and Battle of Arapiles Route&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 34.8-mile (56.1 km) trail, typically takes around 4 hours and 15 minutes. Shorter, easy routes can be completed in under an hour, while longer moderate trails might take 3-4 hours.
tips: On the outskirts of the town of Calvarrasa de Arriba is this historic bullring that is said to date from the 1st century. Regardless of its age, what remains intact are the wonderful views it offers of the Castilian countryside.
Very nice place for history lovers. Highly recommended tour from Salamanca.

Also known as the Greenway between Carbajosa de la Sagrada and Alba de Tormes, this itinerary connects these towns through beautiful landscapes and for the most part the surface is very firm and does not have any difficulty.
A viewpoint with panoramic views of Alba de Tormes. If you don't go through the town, stop at this viewpoint next to the Tormes river.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The estimated time to complete this circular mountain biking route is around 4 hours and 15 minutes. This duration can vary depending on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is considered to be of moderate difficulty. It requires good physical condition and some prior experience with mountain biking. While suitable for intermediate riders, certain sections might demand more advanced skills, and you might even need to push your bike in some parts.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers varied terrain typical of the Salamanca province. You'll encounter a mix of small roads and tracks connecting picturesque medieval towns, passing through cereal fields, holm oak forests (dehesas), riverbanks, and pine forests. Expect both paved and unpaved sections.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the trail?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you can enjoy several notable sights. These include a View of the Castilian countryside from Plaza de Toros de Calvarrasa de Arriba, the Battle of Los Arapiles Monument, and a scenic View of Alba de Tormes and the Historic Stone Bridge.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. Notable sections include parts of the Ruta del Sureste, Vía Verde de la Plata, Ruta Teresiana, and Ruta de la Batalla de Los Arapiles, among others.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: While specific recommendations for this exact route are not available, the Salamanca region generally offers pleasant conditions for mountain biking during spring and autumn. Summers can be hot, and winters might be chilly, so plan accordingly for comfortable riding.

faqs.question: Is the Calvarrasa de Arriba y Alba de Tormes — circular por Peñaranda de Bracamonte route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Information regarding dogs on this specific route is not explicitly stated. However, when cycling in rural areas of Spain, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially near livestock or in populated areas, and to ensure they are well-behaved and don't disturb wildlife.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to use this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there is no mention of permits or entrance fees required to ride this mountain biking trail. It appears to be freely accessible.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: While a specific trailhead parking area isn't detailed, as a circular route passing through Calvarrasa de Arriba, Alba de Tormes, and Peñaranda de Bracamonte, you can likely find suitable parking in or near any of these towns to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through or near towns like Alba de Tormes and Peñaranda de Bracamonte, you will find various options for cafes, restaurants, and accommodation to refuel or stay overnight.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect to see on this mountain bike ride?
faqs.answer: You can expect to immerse yourself in the distinctive rural and natural beauty of the Salamanca region. The route showcases the typical 'dehesa' landscape with centuries-old oak trees, expansive cereal fields, and diverse natural heritage, including riverbanks and pine forests.
